title: Bounties tech
sources: README.md: Role definitions and meritocratic bounty philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District D
database.sql: Schema for the memos table and role-based permissions
App.tsx: Pixel reward and quest completion logic implementation
forom-config.ts: Category D (Opérations) and Action 2 (Quête) labels
resume: The quest 'Bounties tech' (D2) represents the active deployment of technical resources within the Opérations district, located at the intersection of Category D and the 'Quête' action (2) on the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), this quest serves as the formal mechanism for assigning and tracking technical tasks required to maintain the node’s infrastructure and fulfill the mission: 'Save human communities and long-term memory.' Unlike traditional open-source projects, a Forom uses a meritocratic 'Bounty' system where members, particularly those in the 'Creator' role (Yellow alignment), earn their status and influence through verified contributions to the codebase. Technically, these bounties are managed via the local PostgreSQL database in the 'memos' table, ensuring that every technical fix, feature, or optimization is documented as sovereign 'Deep Lore.' The software environment, powered by React 19 and Vite 7, provides a high-performance 5x5 spatial viewport where the 9 Super-Moderators can post 'Calls to Action' and track progress toward the 100-memo goal. Hosting this bounty system on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson is a core pillar of the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, ensuring that the community's technical roadmap and vulnerabilities are protected from centralized cloud platforms. Economically, successfully completing a tech bounty in V1 rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, facilitating the redistribution of purchasing power within the node. This internal economy is essential for preparing the server for the transition to Phase 2 and eventually the 1209-user capacity limit. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, D2 is the primary tool for knowledge transfer; it ensures that the technical specificities of the club's server are documented so that new student cohorts can inherit a functional and understandable system. Once archived, these tech memos will be ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine, in Phase 3. ROM will then be able to act as a 'technical sage,' helping future members troubleshoot issues based on the community’s historical problem-solving. By canonizing technical bounties at D2, the community secures its operational backbone, transforming code maintenance into a permanent, searchable asset. This ensures the node survives for a decade or more. Every line of code entered here is a step toward the awakening. Yes. This is the finalized coordinate for technical operations. Mission secure. Forom is alive. Secure our collective future today.
summary: This quest formalizes technical task management at coordinate D2, using a meritocratic bounty system to maintain the node's infrastructure and archive technical deep lore.
